title: Japanese-Style Masarepa Fritters
description: A tasty fusion of Japanese flavors with the crunchy texture of fritters. These delicious bites are great as a snack or appetizer.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine masarepa, arabian, fritters, briny, saudi, and water.
Mix well until a dough forms.
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Using a cookie scoop or spoon, drop dough into oil, making sure not to overcrowd the skillet.
Cook fritters for 3-4 minutes on each side, or until golden brown.
Remove fritters from oil and place on paper towels to drain excess oil.
Sprinkle with green onions, sesame seeds, and salt to taste.
Note: If desired, you can also shape fritters into desired shapes before frying.
For a crisper exterior, you can chill the dough in the refrigerator for 30 minutes before frying.
Be careful when handling hot oil and fritters.
